subject: Chemistry
title: Vulcanization of Rubber
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Vulcanization of Rubber.

hook.text: Natural rubber melts in summer and cracks in winter. Charles Goodyear accidentally fixed both problems.

buildup.text: Goodyear obsessively experimented for years, going bankrupt multiple times trying to improve rubber.

discovery.text: In 1839, he accidentally dropped rubber mixed with sulfur on a hot stove. It became tough and elastic.

twist.text: Sulfur atoms create cross-links between rubber polymer chains, locking them in place permanently.

climax.text: Vulcanized rubber made tires, hoses, and gaskets possible. The modern industrial world runs on it.

punchline.text: Sulfur cross-links rubber chains. An accident built the tire industry.

quiz.question: What does sulfur do in the vulcanization process?
options: Creates cross-links between rubber polymer chains
Dissolves the rubber into a liquid
